summary: Use single id-shaped Use: placeholders (<design-id>) for designs download / prompt extract / edit upscale instead of pipe-alternative forms (<id|uuid>) so live dogfood's positional resolver recognizes them.
reason: The press's positional placeholder regex (positionalPlaceholderRe = `[<\[]([a-zA-Z][\w-]*)[>\]]`) does not match pipe-alternative usage placeholders like `<id|uuid>` or `<design-id|image-url|file>` — the `|` breaks the token. So extractPositionalPlaceholders returned 0, the get-by-id fixture resolver (added in #3083 / press v4.26.x) never ran, and these three read-by-id commands executed the literal `<design-id>` example against the live API, failing the publish live gate (6/89). Switching the Use line to a single id-shaped token lets the resolver harvest a real id at runtime from the synced store / designs list (designs download) or skip cleanly when there is no list companion (prompt extract, edit upscale) — keeping the live gate green WITHOUT putting a real account id into the public source (preserves artistly-refresh-example-ids). Accepted input types (id/uuid/url/file) remain documented in each command's Long help. Upstream gap: positionalPlaceholderRe should accept `<a|b>` alternatives.
validated_outcome: go build ./... passes; publish live gate (dogfood --live --level full) goes 92/0 (PASS) where it was 83/6 before — designs download/prompt extract/edit upscale now resolve a real id at runtime (no id in source) instead of running the literal placeholder.
